signals.headline: US and Iran trade most intense strikes since ceasefire was extended
signals.summary: The US launched airstrikes against Iran overnight on Thursday, killing at least 14 people, while Tehran responded by hitting Gulf countries in the most intense exchange of fire since a shaky ceasefire was extended last month.
keyTakeaways: After the strikes, the US president posted videos of explosions in Iran and threatened the country once again.
Hours before, he had promised that strikes would not lead to long term fighting but would be “very-fast”.
Significant gaps remain between the two countries over issues such as Iran’s control over the strait of Hormuz, as well as inspections of nuclear facilities.
signals.whyItMatters: The tit-for-tat strikes on Thursday were the largest since Tehran and Washington signed a memorandum of understanding on 17 June aimed at extending the ceasefire and giving space for negotiations for a permanent truce.

ja.headline: 米とイランが停戦延長後、最大規模の応酬
ja.summary: 米軍は木曜日の夜、イランへの空爆を実施し、少なくとも14人が死亡した。イランも湾岸諸国への攻撃で応じ、先月延長された不安定な停戦以降、最も激しい交戦となった。
keyTakeaways: 空爆後、米大統領はイランの爆発映像を投稿し、改めて強硬な姿勢を示した。
数時間前には「長期的な戦闘にはならず、非常に短期間で終わる」と述べていた。
ホルムズ海峡の管理権や核施設の査察など、両国間には依然として大きな隔たりがある。
ja.whyItMatters: 今回の応酬は、6月17日に両国が停戦延長と恒久的な和平交渉の余地を設けることを目的とした覚書に署名して以来、最大規模の衝突となった。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the ceasefire between the US and Iran—it's still holding, right?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It's under serious strain. Overnight Thursday, the US launched airstrikes in Iran that killed at least fourteen people. Iran then responded by striking targets in Gulf countries.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds like more than just a strain. Are we talking about a full breakdown?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Not yet, but these were the most intense strikes since the ceasefire was extended last month. The president posted videos of the explosions and issued new threats afterward.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Wait—he'd said something different earlier, hadn't he?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Hours before the strikes, he promised they'd be very fast and wouldn't lead to long-term fighting.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: What are they actually disagreeing about at this point?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Significant gaps remain—mainly over Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz and inspections of nuclear facilities. Those issues haven't been resolved in negotiations.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the ceasefire agreement from June is still technically in place?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Technically, yes. That memorandum was meant to give space for talks toward a permanent truce, but Thursday's strikes show how fragile that space really is.

signals.headline: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
signals.summary: Democrat Graham Platner has suspended his campaign for US Senate in Maine, following a series of scandals that included a woman accusing him of sexual assault.
keyTakeaways: In the days since the accusation was made, Democrats have called on him to drop out of the race considered pivotal to their chances of gaining control of the US Senate.
In a video posted online, Platner said his decision was not an admission of guilt but due to Democrats' threats to cut off campaign support.
He said he would officially file to withdraw from the race once he's assured his replacement in the race against Republican Susan Collins is selected in a "transparent and democratic" way.
signals.whyItMatters: In June, the New York Times (NYT) reported that Platner had exchanged sexual messages with other women while married, and his wife had warned his campaign of the potential scandal.

ja.headline: 性的暴行疑惑を受け、民主党上院候補が選挙戦を停止
ja.summary: メイン州の民主党上院議員候補グラハム・プラトナーが、女性から性的暴行を告発されたことを含む一連の疑惑を受け、選挙活動の停止を表明した。
keyTakeaways: 告発を受けた数日のうちに、民主党内からも撤退を求める声が相次いだ。上院の過半数奪還を左右する重要な選挙区だけに、党の危機感は強い。
プラトナーは動画の中で、今回の決断は有罪を認めるものではなく、党から支援を打ち切ると脅されたためだと説明した。
共和党のスーザン・コリンズ議員への対抗馬が「透明かつ民主的な」方法で選ばれると確認できた時点で、正式に撤退届を提出するとしている。
ja.whyItMatters: 6月には、プラトナーが既婚中に他の女性と性的なメッセージをやり取りしていたとニューヨーク・タイムズが報じており、妻もその疑惑をあらかじめ選挙陣営に伝えていたという。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Graham Platner suspended his Senate campaign in Maine. What happened?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: A woman accused him of sexual assault. In the days after that, Democrats called on him to drop out of the race.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: This was considered an important race, right?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. Democrats see it as pivotal to their chances of gaining control of the Senate. He's running against Republican Susan Collins.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Did he say why he's suspending now?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: In a video, he said it's not an admission of guilt, but that Democrats threatened to cut off his campaign support.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is he officially out of the race?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Not yet. He says he'll withdraw once he's assured his replacement is selected in a transparent and democratic way.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Were there other issues before this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. In June, the New York Times reported he'd exchanged sexual messages with other women while married, and his wife had warned his campaign about it.

signals.headline: EU’s ‘unpleasant and dangerous’ border checks need overhaul, says Greek airports boss
signals.summary: The boss of 14 Greek airports has called for a serious overhaul of the EU’s new border checks after having to erect gazebos for passengers queuing outside.
keyTakeaways: Zinell’s airports have had to use gazebos to shield queuing passengers from the sun as they wait to be processed, while vulnerable people have been prioritised through security to ensure their safety.
Greek authorities have indicated that police will not check UK passengers, although there is no blanket legal exemption from the biometric tests.
Visitors from the UK make up the bulk of non-EU tourists passing through Zinell’s airports, which include Corfu, Rhodes, Mykonos and Crete.
signals.whyItMatters: Iata said last week that passengers were also experiencing “delays and missed connections” in Portugal, Spain, Italy and Belgium, while Ryanair warned of “queue chaos” in airports including at popular holiday destinations such as Alicante, Málaga and Palma.

ja.headline: EUの新たな入国審査、ギリシャ空港トップが見直しを訴える
ja.summary: ギリシャの14空港を運営するトップが、EUの新しい入国審査制度の抜本的な見直しを求めた。審査待ちの旅客が屋外に列をなす事態を受け、空港側はテントを設置して対応を余儀なくされている。
keyTakeaways: 審査待ちの列が屋外に延び、日差しを避けるためのテントを設置。体の弱い旅客は安全確保のため優先的に通過させている。
ギリシャ当局は英国人旅客への生体認証検査を実施しない方針を示しているが、法的な免除規定は存在しない。
コルフ島やロードス島、ミコノス島、クレタ島などを抱えるこれらの空港では、EU域外からの旅客の大半を英国人が占める。
ja.whyItMatters: 国際航空運送協会は先週、ポルトガル・スペイン・イタリア・ベルギーでも遅延や乗り継ぎ失敗が相次いでいると指摘しており、混乱はギリシャにとどまらない。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I heard there's some kind of problem with border checks in Greece?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes. The boss of 14 Greek airports is calling for an overhaul of the EU's new border checks. They've had to put up gazebos outside because passengers are queuing in the sun waiting to be processed.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Gazebos? That sounds pretty chaotic.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. They're also prioritizing vulnerable people through security to keep them safe. The airport boss described the situation as unpleasant and dangerous.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is this just happening in Greece?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: No. An airline industry group reported delays and missed connections last week in Portugal, Spain, Italy, and Belgium. Ryanair warned of queue chaos at popular destinations like Alicante, Málaga, and Palma.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Are UK travelers affected by this?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: UK passengers make up most of the non-EU tourists at these Greek airports, which include Corfu, Rhodes, Mykonos, and Crete. Greek authorities have indicated police won't check UK passengers, though there's no official legal exemption from the biometric tests.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the new system just isn't working smoothly yet.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's what it looks like. The calls for an overhaul suggest these aren't just temporary teething problems.

signals.headline: Get a $30 credit when you reserve Samsung’s upcoming Galaxy phones
signals.summary: Samsung is announcing new foldables soon, and you can save $30 if you register your interest ahead of time.
keyTakeaways: Even though they haven’t been officially announced yet, Samsung is giving you a chance to save some cash when you preorder what we’re expecting to be the brand’s updated Galaxy Z Fold phones.
If you register your interest ahead of time and end up preordering one of the new phones shortly after they’re announced, Samsung will give you a $30 store credit at checkout.
You have to use the credit when you preorder the phone.
signals.whyItMatters: There are no downsides to registering your interest, so if you think you might be interested in buying one of the upcoming phones, it’s worth filling out the form .

ja.headline: サムスンの新型Galaxyを事前登録すると30ドルのクレジットがもらえる
ja.summary: サムスンが近く新しい折りたたみスマートフォンを発表する見通しだ。正式発表前に関心登録をしておくと、予約購入時に30ドル分のストアクレジットを受け取れる。
keyTakeaways: 正式発表前でも、Galaxy Z Foldシリーズの新モデルとみられる端末の予約割引に備えられる。
事前に関心登録を済ませ、発表後に予約注文すると、購入時に30ドルのストアクレジットが適用される。
クレジットは予約注文の際に使用する必要がある。
ja.whyItMatters: 登録に特段のデメリットはなく、購入を少しでも検討しているなら、フォームに記入しておく価値はある。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: I saw Samsung is offering some kind of credit for their new phones. What's going on?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Samsung has new foldable Galaxy phones coming soon, and they're offering a thirty dollar store credit if you register your interest ahead of time.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So the phones haven't even been announced yet?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Right. They're expected to be updated versions of the Galaxy Z Fold phones, but Samsung is letting people sign up early.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: How does the credit actually work?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: You register now, then when the phones are officially announced, you'd preorder one and get the thirty dollar credit applied at checkout.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Do I have to use that credit right away?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, you have to use it when you preorder the phone. It's not something you can save for later.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Is there any catch to registering?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: No downside. You're just filling out a form to show interest. So if you're even thinking about buying one, it's worth doing.

signals.headline: Meta is reportedly working on smart glasses that would be recording all the time
signals.summary: The ‘super sensing’ glasses could continuously record audio and frequently snap photos.
keyTakeaways: The company is working on prototype “super sensing” always-aware smart glasses that could continuously record audio and snap photos “every few seconds,” according to the Financial Times .
The wearer could then ask Meta AI about the captured audio and images.
Instead, the metadata from that audio and images would be extracted and uploaded to the server for Meta’s AI to query, which proponents argue would have fewer privacy implications.
signals.whyItMatters: Meta might be the next company to make an always-on AI wearable.

ja.headline: メタ、常時録音・撮影するスマートグラスを開発中か
ja.summary: メタが「超感知」型スマートグラスの試作に取り組んでいると報じられた。音声を継続的に録音し、数秒おきに写真を撮影する機能を備える可能性があるという。
keyTakeaways: フィナンシャル・タイムズによると、常時周囲を認識し、音声録音と写真撮影を数秒間隔で行うプロトタイプを開発中
収集した音声や画像をもとに、装着者がメタのAIに問い合わせることができる仕組み
実際にはデータそのものではなくメタデータをサーバーに送信する方式で、プライバシーへの影響を抑えられると主張する声もある
ja.whyItMatters: 常時稼働型AIウェアラブルの実用化に、メタが踏み込もうとしている。

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: So Meta is working on smart glasses that record all the time?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Yes, they're developing prototype glasses they call 'super sensing.' According to the Financial Times, these would continuously record audio and snap photos every few seconds.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Every few seconds? What would happen to all that footage?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: The idea is you could ask Meta AI about what you've captured—like searching through your day by voice.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: That sounds like a lot of data being sent to Meta's servers.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: Actually, they say the full audio and images wouldn't be uploaded. Instead, metadata would be extracted and sent to the server for the AI to query.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: And that's supposed to be more private?

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: That's what proponents argue—that using metadata instead of raw recordings would have fewer privacy implications.

captions.speaker: listener
captions.text: Still, always-on recording feels like a big step.

captions.speaker: explainer
captions.text: It is. If this moves forward, Meta would be joining the race to make always-on AI wearables.
